_README: Planning targets and WARNING THRESHOLDS for teacher workload — NOT absolute truth. They only help flag possible problems. All hours are per ACADEMIC YEAR. Definitions: 'course-work hours' = ALL course-related work (planning, teaching, preparation, feedback, meetings, evaluation, communication, etc.); 'in-class hours' = scheduled teaching sessions only (what the calendar measures), one part of course-work. STUDENT workload (~135 h / 5 ECTS) is a different thing and is shown for reference only — do not confuse it with teacher workload. Edit any value below; absent teachers fall back to the defaults.
_note_coursework: 5 ECTS ≈ 100 teacher course-work hours (20 h per ECTS).
_note_inclass: Per 5 ECTS: normal in-class ≈ 50–60 h, warn if < 40 h or > 70 h. Values here are PER ECTS.
_note_student: 5 ECTS ≈ 135 h of STUDENT work (info/reference only).
_note_yearly: A full working year ≈ 1600 h; a reasonable yearly COURSE-WORK target is ~800–1200 h (not in-class hours). Warn clearly below/above. Used for PLANNED bookings.
_note_realized: REALIZED view: count BOOKED hours only (overlapping bookings count once — the longer one). Compare a teacher's booked COURSE hours to this range. Admin/meeting hours are kept separate and are NOT included in this target. No per-teacher course-work estimate is made for realized data.
_note_teachers: Per teacher you can set 'fte' (scales the default yearly target range, e.g. 0.5 -> 400–600 h) or set explicit 'yearly_low' and 'yearly_high' to override entirely.
